audience: people who work at a computer all day; recurring weekly workflows
note: Panels draw all 10 testers from this roster, adapting each persona's 'motivation' to the app under test. Retire/replace personas only via Elaine.
app: TripSync — no-login collaborative visual trip calendar: name a trip, get a secret share link, paste a doc-style itinerary that auto-parses into a month/week/day-hourly calendar; a friend opens the link with no login, edits/adds/confirms events, and one-clicks any event into Google Calendar.

personas.name: Priya
personas.stack: terminal + neovim, GitHub, Kubernetes dashboards, Datadog, Linear, Slack
personas.temperament: skeptical of new tools; hates signups; will inspect network tab if suspicious; abandons anything slower than a CLI
personas.motivation: Priya is driving up to a friend's cabin for a long weekend and wants to drop the loose plan from the group chat into something her non-technical friends can open on their phones without making an account or installing anything.

personas.name: Marcus
personas.stack: VS Code, GitHub, Vercel, Figma dev mode, Storybook, Discord/Slack
personas.temperament: enthusiastic early adopter; shares tools in team Slack when impressed; notices janky CSS instantly
personas.motivation: Marcus is hosting two college friends visiting next month and is tired of the weekend plan living in a Google Doc nobody opens on their phone, so he wants a share link that turns into a clean visual calendar they can actually glance at.

personas.name: Wen
personas.stack: BigQuery/SQL, Google Sheets, Looker Studio, dbt, GA4, Slack
personas.temperament: lives in data hygiene; distrusts tools that transform data invisibly; wants CSV in/out everywhere
personas.motivation: Wen already has a tidy day-by-day itinerary typed up for a family reunion trip and wants to paste it in once and have it parse correctly into a calendar without her having to re-enter every time and place by hand.

personas.name: Tomás
personas.role: Operations analyst at a mid-size company
personas.stack: Excel (power user), Tableau, Jira, Teams, SharePoint
personas.temperament: pragmatic; IT blocks installs so browser tools appeal; wary of pasting company data into random sites
personas.motivation: Tomás is coordinating a multi-family beach house week and needs a single browser link he can send to relatives of all ages who will never sign up for yet another app or download anything.

personas.name: Dana
personas.device: MacBook in cafes; phone between meetings
personas.stack: HubSpot, GA4, Notion, Canva, LinkedIn Campaign Manager, Google Ads
personas.temperament: ruthless about time; if value isn't obvious in one scroll she's gone; screenshots tools she likes for the team channel
personas.motivation: Dana is planning a girls' weekend and wants the itinerary to look as polished as the Canva mood board she made for it, plus let everyone tap an event straight into their own Google Calendar so nobody misses the brunch reservation.

personas.name: Jules
personas.stack: Notion, Buffer, Mailchimp, Figma (consuming), X/LinkedIn/Mastodon, Discord
personas.temperament: posts constantly, juggles platform quirks daily; allergic to anything requiring a login for a small job
personas.motivation: Jules is organizing a friends' camping trip and refuses to make everyone create an account just to see when they're leaving Friday, so a no-login link that opens to a visual schedule is exactly the small-job tool they want.

personas.name: Aisha
personas.stack: Figma, FigJam, Notion, Loom, Maze, Slack
personas.temperament: judges craft hard — spacing, copy tone, empty states; a clumsy UI loses her regardless of utility; advocates loudly for tools that feel considered
personas.motivation: Aisha is planning a city trip with her partner and wants the day-hourly view to actually look beautiful and considered on her phone instead of the ugly bulleted Notion page she usually ends up squinting at on the train.

personas.name: Rob
personas.stack: Figma, Illustrator, Photoshop, Google Drive, client Slacks/emails
personas.temperament: does repetitive asset-prep grunt work weekly; price-sensitive; compares everything to 'I could do this in Photoshop in 4 minutes'
personas.motivation: Rob is splitting a ski-trip rental with three friends and wants to send one link where everyone can add their own arrival times and confirm what they're in for, instead of him manually maintaining the master plan everyone ignores.

personas.name: Elena
personas.device: laptop + phone, half the day in meetings
personas.stack: Google Calendar (wall-to-wall), Slack, Linear, Google Docs, Zoom, dashboards she skims
personas.temperament: 30-second patience budget; uses tools between meetings on her phone; anything requiring setup is dead to her; recommends tools that save her reports time
personas.motivation: Elena lives in Google Calendar and is visiting family for a long weekend, so the killer feature is glancing at the trip plan on her phone between meetings and one-tapping each event into the calendar that already runs her life.

personas.name: Sam
personas.stack: Notion, Sheets, Slack, Zoom, Asana, Amplitude (skims), endless browser tabs
personas.temperament: synthesizes and shares constantly — exports, links, screenshots; values anything that makes him look organized; mild tech confidence, won't debug anything
personas.motivation: Sam is the friend who always ends up organizing the bachelor-party weekend and wants a single shareable link that makes him look organized — paste the rough plan in, send it out, and let everyone confirm and export to their own calendars.
